title: A shared baseline
body: The comparable series begins in the early twentieth century, when both countries were marked by high pretax inequality.

title: France after war
body: France shows a sharp compression around World War II and the immediate postwar settlement.

title: The US turn upward
body: US pretax inequality rises more visibly from the late twentieth century onward.

title: Divergence opens
body: The two countries increasingly separate, with the US moving above France by a wider margin.

title: The gap as its own story
body: The purple line tracks the US-France Gini difference, making the widening distance easier to see.

title: A persistent divide
body: By the end of the available series, the comparative inequality gap remains a central feature of the data.
